text.title: gha-workflow-structure Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gha-workflow-structure. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Design the job dependency graph (needs: relationships) as a DAG BEFORE writing YAML. Jobs without needs: run in parallel; jobs with needs: run after their dependencies. Never serialize jobs that can run in parallel.
rule.rationale: Sequential jobs on GHA-hosted runners waste wall-clock time and runner-minutes. A lint + test + build chain that could be lint → (test || build) in parallel cuts CI time by 30-50%.

rule.statement: Concurrency groups for PR/push CI workflows MUST use group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }} with cancel-in-progress: true. Concurrency groups for deployment workflows MUST use group: deploy-${{ inputs.environment }} with cancel-in-progress: false.
rule.rationale: A shared global concurrency group cancels production deploys when a new commit to a feature branch triggers a CI run with the same group name. Per-ref grouping for CI and per-environment grouping for deploys prevents cross-contamination.

rule.statement: Every scheduled workflow MUST also have a workflow_dispatch trigger so operators can run it manually outside the cron schedule during incidents.
rule.rationale: A scheduled backup or security scan that only runs at 02:00 UTC cannot be manually triggered after an incident that happened at 14:00. workflow_dispatch is a zero-cost safety valve.
